g.text: Project dashboard · Command cards · Env selector · Log peek · Status bar

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What IPC channels connect the Electron renderer to the main process for this product')
sendPrompt('How does the env selector dropdown work — what data does it show and how does it apply the selection')
sendPrompt('What does the command card component look like in detail — play button, log peek, terminal icon, status badge, uptime counter')
sendPrompt('What does the global status bar show — running process count, warnings, env health across all projects')

g.text: The brain. Owns all state. Survives window close. Restores on app restart.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain the command discovery engine in full detail — every file it reads, the priority order, how it infers command intent and assigns categories')
sendPrompt('Explain the env resolver — how it detects varlock, plain .env, and raw shell env, and builds the correct injection command for each')
sendPrompt('Explain the process registry — the JSON state it stores per process, how it maps to tmux session names, and how it restores after app restart')

g.text: always runs before the process starts

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Show the exact shell command that wraps a user command with varlock when varlock is detected in the project')
sendPrompt('What happens for projects that use plain .env files without varlock — how does the env resolver inject the variables')
sendPrompt('What is the env snapshot — how is a copy of the resolved env saved at process start time so the user can inspect it later')

g.text: OS owns these — not the app

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Show the exact tmux commands used to create a named detached session, capture output, check if running, and kill cleanly')
sendPrompt('What is node-pty used for — reading live output from tmux into the Electron app without polling')
sendPrompt('How does the OS terminal open command work — the exact commands for macOS Terminal, iTerm2, Windows Terminal, and Linux gnome-terminal to attach to a named tmux session')

g.text: reads and writes — never reimplements logic

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What files does the filesystem layer read for command discovery and what exactly does it extract from each')
sendPrompt('What does the .vibestart.json project config file store — saved commands, env preferences, last used env, team settings')
sendPrompt('What does chokidar watch for — which file changes trigger re-discovery and which trigger re-validation')
sendPrompt('What goes in the SQLite database — process history, project configs, env selections, uptime records')
sendPrompt('What is stored in the app config — user preferences, default terminal app, tmux vs screen fallback, theme')
sendPrompt('How does the process registry restore state on app startup — reading tmux list-sessions and matching against saved records')

svg.text: Varlock boundary — everything below this line is Varlock's problem, never reimplemented above it
validate · resolve secrets · inject env · scan leaks · type-check

g.text: varlock load --format=json · varlock run -- cmd · varlock scan · varlock init
